content.problemStatement:
Chef has to cross a highway with infinite length consisting of NNN parallel lanes. The width of each lane is YYY. Between each pair of adjacent lanes, there is a divider with negligible width; at each divider and the side of the highway, Chef can wait for an arbitrarily long time to allow the cars to pass by and then continue crossing the highway. Since Chef is lazy, he wants to cross the highway while walking the minimum possible distance; therefore, he will only move in a straight line perpendicular to the highway. Chef walks with a constant velocity SSS.

There is exactly one car in each lane. Each car is as wide as a whole lane (so it covers the entire width of its lane), has a known length and travels with fixed velocity in either direction along the highway. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the car in the iii-th lane has velocity ViV_iVi‚Äč and length CiC_iCi‚Äč.

Let's measure distances along the highway, i.e. all points which lie on the same line perpendicular to the highway have the same coordinate xxx. Chef's position is x=0x=0x=0; for each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the initial position of the car in the iii-th lane is PiP_iPi‚Äč and the direction in which it's moving is DiD_iDi‚Äč. If Di=1D_i = 1Di‚Äč=1, the car is moving in the positive direction (its position is increasing) and if Di=0D_i = 0Di‚Äč=0, the car is moving in the negative direction (its position is decreasing).

The drivers on a highway don't expect anyone to be crossing it, so they drive carelessly and won't be able to apply brakes at any point in time.

Find the minimum time Chef needs to cross the highway without getting hit by a car. Chef does not get hit by a car if the minimum distance between him and any car remains greater than 10‚ąí610^{-6}10‚ąí6 at all times.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, SSS and YYY denoting the number of lanes, Chef's walking speed and the width of a lane.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers V1,V2,‚Ä¶,VNV_1, V_2, \dots, V_NV1‚Äč,V2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,VN‚Äč denoting the absolute velocities of the cars.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers D1,D2,‚Ä¶,DND_1, D_2, \dots, D_ND1‚Äč,D2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,DN‚Äč denoting the directions in which the cars are moving.
The fourth line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \dots, P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč denoting the initial positions of the cars. The position of a car is the xxx-coordinate of its front side, i.e. for a car moving in the positive direction, it is the largest coordinate of any point of the car, and for a car moving in the negative direction, it is the smallest coordinate of any point of the car.
The fifth line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \dots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč denoting the lengths of the cars.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number ‚ÄĒ the minimum time needed to cross the highway. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute error does not exceed 10‚ąí310^{-3}10‚ąí3.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef has to wait at the side of the highway and allow the first car to pass. As soon as the car passes by, he can reach the first divider. At this divider, he has to wait again for the second car to pass. Chef's situation is drawn in the image.

Example case 2: We see that Chef needs time at least 1778=22.125\frac{177}{8} = 22.1258177‚Äč=22.125 to cross the highway, but the car would arrive at Chef's position at an earlier time 22.110522.110522.1105. Hence, Chef must wait for the car to pass. Also, since Chef must wait until the car passes completely, he will wait for 4222190=22.221053\frac{4222}{190}=22.2210531904222‚Äč=22.221053 before starting to cross the lane. Hence, the total time taken to cross the highway is 44.34605344.34605344.346053.
Example case 3: Chef can cross the road in time 100010001000, but at that time, the car will reach x=0x=0x=0. Hence, at that moment, the distance between Chef and the car will be 000, which is less than 10‚ąí610^{-6}10‚ąí6. Therefore, Chef has to wait for the car to pass before crossing.
